title: astracantha rousseana
intro: Astracantha rousseana, also known as Caragana rousseana, is a species of shrub or small tree that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, specifically in Kazakhstan and Kyrgyzstan. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, and is traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Astracantha rousseana are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that are believed to have a range of health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function.
